fields.name: tenantId
fields.whyRequired: Identifies the Entra tenant whose users may sign in; embedded in the OIDC authority URL.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: OpenID configuration JSON returned with issuer matching the tenant; HTTP 200.

fields.name: clientId
fields.whyRequired: Identifies the LocalAIFactory app registration during the OIDC authorization code flow.
fields.validationCommand: az ad app show --id client-id-guid-placeholder --query appId
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: Returns the same client id; app registration exists in the tenant.

fields.name: clientSecretReference
fields.exampleValue: vault://example-kv/secrets/laf-entra-client-secret#latest (REFERENCE ONLY — no secret value in repo)
fields.realProvider: Operator secrets manager (Azure Key Vault / Windows DPAPI key ring)
fields.whyRequired: The confidential-client secret is required to redeem the authorization code; it MUST live in a vault and be referenced, never stored inline (CLAUDE.md §10).
fields.validationCommand: az keyvault secret show --vault-name example-kv --name laf-entra-client-secret --query id
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: Secret reference resolves in the vault; value is never printed; app retrieves it at runtime via the reference.

fields.name: authority
fields.whyRequired: OIDC middleware uses the authority to discover endpoints and validate the issuer/signing keys.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: issuer matches https://login.microsoftonline.com/tenant-id-guid-placeholder/v2.0 (or the tenant's STS issuer).

fields.name: redirectUri
fields.realProvider: Operator Entra app registration (Web redirect URIs)
fields.whyRequired: Entra only returns the authorization code to a pre-registered redirect URI; must match exactly.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: Redirect URI list contains https://laf.example.local/signin-oidc exactly.

fields.name: postLogoutRedirectUri
fields.whyRequired: Enables clean single sign-out back to the app after the Entra logout.

fields.name: claimsMapping
fields.realProvider: Operator Entra app roles + LocalAIFactory role mapping configuration
fields.whyRequired: Entra app-role claims must map deterministically onto LocalAIFactory's UserRole enum so authorization policies work.
fields.validationCommand: Sign in with a test user and inspect the issued id_token roles claim; compare to the configured mapping table.
fields.expectedSuccessfulOutput: A user assigned the Entra 'Admin' app role is granted LocalAIFactory UserRole.Admin; unmapped users receive the least-privilege default.
